hero_subtitle: SFTY connects screening providers and compliance rules to help VASPs identify and manage politically exposed person risks during onboarding with clear audit trail and case workflows.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Pep risk remains hidden in onboarding
problem_description: Crypto compliance teams struggle to identify politically exposed persons early due to fragmented screening outputs and disconnected provider data. This creates gaps in risk visibility that slow onboarding and weaken case review readiness for AML oversight.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates PEP screening results, identity data and risk rules into one operational workflow that powers onboarding decisions, case reviews and audit-ready evidence for crypto compliance teams.
workflow_title: From screening hits to case escalation
workflow_description: SFTY processes sanctions and PEP screening results by routing alerts into structured cases for review, decision, evidence collection and compliance reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One approval evidence layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects PEP screening outputs with workflows, case management, decisions and audit evidence, enabling clearer control and traceability across crypto AML compliance operations.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs requiring a structured layer for PEP risk orchestration, audit-ready evidence and controlled deployment in complex compliance operations.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY about managing PEP screening in crypto AML operations
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how PEP screening integrates with onboarding workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports PEP screening decisions

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for crypto compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects PEP screening results with AML workflows, enabling structured reviews, case management and audit evidence for VASPs managing onboarding and ongoing political exposure risk.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support PEP screening workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes PEP alerts through policy-driven workflows, linking screening results to cases and escalation paths while supporting human review and decision logging.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve PEP screening evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence by recording screening outputs, decision logs and case history to support structured internal reviews and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates provider outputs into a connected compliance layer without disrupting existing tools, allowing seamless orchestration across KYC, KYB, KYT, and screening systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions or screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above sanctions and screening providers, connecting their outputs into structured compliance workflows without replacing those core services.
final_title: Structure crypto PEP screening workflows clearly
final_description: Review how SFTY connects screening results, compliance rules and cases into one controlled PEP risk workflow.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
